events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes shaft alignment work in progress with a coupling gap out of spec. I need to evaluate the procedure sequence and determine what must happen before work can continue.

Key facts:
- Shaft alignment work, in-progress
- Observed condition: coupling gap out of spec
- This means the alignment is not correct - the coupling gap is outside acceptable tolerances
- Component condition: the coupling/alignment is non-compliant, possibly installed-defective

The coupling gap being out of spec means the alignment work cannot proceed to completion/acceptance until corrected. The work must stop and correct the alignment.

Decision: fail (non-compliant condition)
Risk: high (misalignment can cause equipment damage, vibration, bearing failure)
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: maybe 40-50% (alignment in progress but not correct)
Value: 0 (no specific numeric reading given)
Confidence: 0.85
Order: proper sequence would be rough alignment, then precision alignment with laser, then check coupling gap, then final verification
Findings: coupling gap out of spec, alignment not within tolerance
Actions: stop work, correct alignment, re-measure coupling gap
References: API 686, laser system manuals

    "Coupling gap measured out of spec during active shaft alignment",

  "actions": [
    "Stop alignment work at current step",
    "Re-check soft foot and shimming before further correction",
    "Correct vertical and horizontal angular/offset alignment using laser system",
    "Re-measure coupling gap until within spec before proceeding to final torque",
    "Document corrected readings per quality gate"
  ],
  "rationale": "An out-of-spec coupling gap indicates the shaft alignment is non-compliant. Per API 686 and laser system procedures, the gap must be corrected and verified before work can advance to final torque and acceptance. Proceeding would install a defective condition.",

}
